name: Inoreader RSS and News Reader API
what_it_does: RSS and news aggregation platform with REST API — enabling power users to follow unlimited RSS feeds, aggregate content by keywords and topics, apply automation rules (auto-tag, star, broadcast), send articles to external services, and monitor web page changes, with a developer API compatible with Google Reader API pattern for broad client integration.
use_cases: Subscribing to RSS feeds and querying article stream via Inoreader API for content pipeline
Applying automation rules to incoming articles via Inoreader API for intelligent content routing
Sending filtered Inoreader articles to external services (Evernote, Pocket, Zapier) via API
Monitoring web pages for changes via Inoreader web monitoring feature triggered via API
Building custom news readers using Inoreader as backend feed aggregation service via API
not_for: AI-powered market intelligence (use Feedly Pro+ for Leo AI topic tracking)
Bookmark management beyond RSS (use Raindrop.io for visual bookmark management)
Team collaborative knowledge bases (Inoreader is personal/small team reader)
best_when: A power RSS user needs a high-capacity, automation-friendly reader with web page monitoring — especially as a Feedly alternative with Google Reader API compatibility for existing client apps and Zapier/Make automation.
avoid_when: You primarily need AI-powered filtering, social media monitoring, or team-scale content collaboration beyond sharing articles.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code flow. Bearer token auth. User-scoped access. Webhook for article event forwarding (rules-based). REST API v1 with Google Reader API compatibility (original Reader API pattern). Scoped by access level (read, write, subscriptions). Team collaboration API for Enterprise. Integration with Zapier, IFTTT, Make, and 100+ apps. Rules engine for automation (auto-forward, tag, mark-read).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 150 feed subscriptions, limited automation rules
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro $7.99/month; Pro+ $14.99/month (unlimited feeds, full automation, rules, web monitoring); Teams custom
pricing.notes: Bulgarian company (Sofia). Founded 2013. Bootstrapped and profitable. Founded by two brothers (Yordan and Lyubomir Yanchev). 500,000+ active users. Strong power user community. Google Reader API compatibility makes it work with hundreds of existing RSS client apps. Unlimited feeds on Pro is key differentiator vs. Feedly's 1,000 source limit on Pro. Web page monitoring (non-RSS content) is unique feature. Automation rules engine for sophisticated content routing. GDPR compliant as EU company.

performance.rate_limits: API calls per hour based on plan tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API v1 with Google Reader API compatibility. OAuth2 with scopes. Good documentation with automation rule examples. Webhook for rules-based article forwarding. Rate limits per plan tier but not fully documented. Bulgarian company with GDPR compliance. Google Reader API compatibility means broad existing client support.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Article reads fully idempotent. Stream queries idempotent by feed + pagination. Subscription reads idempotent. Mark-as-read idempotent by article ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limits vary by plan tier — not fully documented; Pro plan needed for API access
Google Reader API compatibility adds legacy endpoint complexity alongside native API
Web page monitoring (non-RSS) has check interval limitations by plan
Automation rules run server-side — complex rule debugging requires UI
Bulgarian company — verify EU data residency requirements for specific data types

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. EU/Bulgarian data residency. OAuth2 with scopes. Small bootstrapped team with privacy-first EU culture. Reading habits and subscriptions are privacy-sensitive — protect OAuth tokens. Webhook forwarding sends article content to external services — validate webhook destinations. Automation rules run on server — rules content may expose monitoring strategy.
